content.claim: In segregated schools where Romani students exceed 75%, institutional segregation may prevent SEN expert assessment referrals, whereas in schools with 50–75% Romani pupils, children are more likely to be tested, diagnosed with SEN, and referred to segregated special classes; concurrently, socially deprived and uneducated parents often lack awareness of procedural rights during assessments without support personnel.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Geographic scope and sampling method of field research not fully specified in retrieved excerpt
Pattern documented but causal direction between segregation and SEN classification not definitively established
Findings suggest SEN classification rates cannot be read as pure prevalence signals in ethnically mixed catchment areas
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
